ads.author: Craig Kurtzner
ads.title: Area 51 / Maximum Force Atari arcade game
ads.description: This is a commercial Atari duo light gun game from 1998 that plays both Area 51 and Maximum force. These games are a blast to play because you can play cooperatively to shoot bad guys and defeat stages. While doing so you are also playing vs each other to see who can get the highest score. There are also a bunch of power ups and secret rooms you can find by shooting objects in the background. Ok, enough of the game play... let’s get down to the arcade machine itself. It came out of a couple’s personal collection and was well cared for. The guns are dead on and clean. The keys for the coin door and for the back of the machine are present. The coin mechs are working. The crt monitor is clear and bright. The original hard drive has been replaced with a solid state drive. The cabinet is in good shape. Overall this is a solid arcade game in excellent working condition. Please feel free to reach out if you are interested or have any questions. Game on

It has been stored in doors in a smoke free and pet free home and features no cosmetic damage. Please note the Home Edition does not accept quarters, it stays on Free Play, just press the Start button to begin. Up to 4 players can play against each other and the game supports various multi-ball modes. Game is for local pickup only at my address, I cannot ship it or deliver it as I do not own a truck. Buyer can test the game and make sure they are satisfied with it before purchase, but once it leaves my house, all sales are final. Payment accepted through Cash App. Dimensions: 70 inches high, 28 inches wide, and 53 inches deep. The legs can be removed and the head of the machine can be folded down so that it can fit in the back of most vans or any truck. Machine weighs approximately 220 pounds.

ads.author: Patrick DeCristo
ads.title: Fender Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster
ads.description: Fender Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster $1549 + shipping *No Trades Please* Fender’s initial release consisted of only 65 guitars worldwide, and they sold out within minutes of launch. This limited edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster is in mint condition and was only removed from the box for testing and photography. There are no cosmetic blemishes to note anywhere on the instrument. Includes the original Fender gig bag, factory paperwork, and will be shipped in the original Fender shipping box. Created in collaboration with Bandai Namco, this incredibly limited Fender x PAC-MAN release celebrates one of the most iconic video games of all time with a bold, collectible twist on the legendary Telecaster platform. Designed to bridge generations of players and collectors alike, this model combines unmistakable PAC-MAN nostalgia with the modern playability and performance of the Player II Series. Whether you’re a serious collector, lifelong gamer, or working musician looking for something truly unique, this Telecaster delivers both visual impact and professional-level functionality. The guitar features a comfortable Modern “C” neck profile, a 9.5” radius fingerboard with rolled edges, and 22 medium jumbo frets for smooth, effortless playability. The body showcases vibrant PAC-MAN arcade graphics on the front, while the classic PAC-MAN logo adorns the back, making this one of the most visually distinctive Fender collaborations ever produced. Equipped with Player Series Alnico V Single-Coil Telecaster pickups, this guitar produces the classic Tele tone players expect — crisp highs, punchy mids, and tight, articulate lows. A 3-way blade switch, 6-saddle bridge, and Fender ClassicGear tuners provide reliable tuning stability and versatile tonal control for both studio and stage use. Features include: Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN collaboration Extremely limited initial run of only 65 guitars Modern “C” maple neck 9.5” fingerboard radius with rolled edges 22 medium jumbo frets Player Series Alnico V Telecaster single-coil pickups 3-way blade switch 6-saddle Telecaster bridge Fender ClassicGear tuners PAC-MAN artwork on front and logo on back Original Fender gig bag and paperwork included A rare and highly collectible collaboration that perfectly blends arcade nostalgia with classic Fender craftsmanship.

ads.author: Kenneth Alan
ads.title: StarBlade – Sega Mega-CD / Sega CD – Namco 1994 Japan NTSC-J – Rare Shooter Game
ads.description: Original Japanese release of Starblade for the Sega Mega-CD / Sega CD, released in 1994 by Namco. This classic sci-fi space shooter is known for its cinematic presentation, scaling effects, and arcade-style gameplay. Originally an impressive arcade title, StarBlade became one of the standout shooter experiences available on CD-based systems during the 1990s. A highly collectible release for Sega CD and retro shooter fans. Its timeless design and historical value make it a standout addition to any collection. Delve into the golden age of arcade gaming with this classic non-video arcade machine. The year 1925 marks a period when arcade games were the epitome of social entertainment, and this piece embodies that era. Whether you're a seasoned collector or new to the world of vintage arcade games, the Challenger offers a glimpse into a bygone period of fun and skill. Others selling for over 1k.
